headline: Ukraine peace-window emergency re-queue: Russia and Ukraine have declared COMPETING (not coordinated) ceasefire frameworks. Putin declared unilateral ceasefire May 8-9 (Victory Day) with threat of 'massive missile strike on Kyiv' if violated. Zelenskyy declined Russia's framing and declared Ukraine's own ceasefire starting 00:00 May 5-6, calling Russia's proposal 'not serious.' This is the most significant diplomatic signal since the October ceasefire but is NOT a sustained framework. Per methodology v1.2 bad-faith ceasefire rule, declared cessation concurrent with active threats and lack of coordination is an inverted signal — Russia's declaration is in particular conditional on Ukraine compliance with terms Russia is unilaterally setting. Ukraine's own framework declaration is a modest AWR/INT positive (proactive humanitarian-window proposal independent of adversary) but this is signal-level, not resolution-level. Composite 50.0 (published) → 50.0 (sustained). Sub-threshold delta. Functional band sustained. May 9-10 verifies whether either ceasefire holds — emergency re-queue automatic post-Victory Day.
key_evidence: May 4 2026: Putin declared unilateral ceasefire May 8-9 (Victory Day), threatening 'massive missile strike on Kyiv' if Ukraine violates it.
May 4-5 2026: Zelenskyy declined Russia's framing and declared Ukraine's own ceasefire starting 00:00 May 5-6 night, calling Russia's proposal 'not serious.'
Both sides nominally ceasing fire but for different periods and with different framings. Coordinated cessation has NOT been achieved.
Per methodology v1.2: bad-faith ceasefire pattern (declared cessation concurrent with violations or threats) treats unilateral declarations as inverted/limited signals.
Ukraine's own framework declaration is a modest AWR/INT positive (proactive humanitarian-window proposal) but signal-level, not resolution.
Cumulative evidence base sustained: UN HRMMU monitoring; Ombudsperson office active; HRW 2026 World Report; ongoing wartime governance with civil-society engagement.

evidence.finding: Russia unilaterally declares May 8-9 Victory Day ceasefire; Zelenskyy tables own truce.
Russia and Ukraine declare competing ceasefires; not coordinated; different windows.
Putin announces 2-day ceasefire with explicit threat of 'massive missile strike on Kyiv' if violated.
Russia declares truce; Ukraine response framing.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 unchanged. Ukraine's proactive ceasefire-window declaration is a modest awareness-of-civilian-suffering signal but does not move the dimension because the peace-window has not yet held in practice. Sustained signal awaits May 9-10 verification.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 unchanged. Russia's threat of 'massive missile strike' even within its own declared ceasefire window is a counter-party boundary problem, not Ukraine's; Ukraine's own ceasefire declaration is a boundary-honor signal but not yet operationally tested.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 unchanged. No structural-governance change in window.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 unchanged. Ukraine's declination of Russia's framing while offering an alternative framework is an integrity signal but signal-level, not resolution-level.

negativeSignals: Russia's threat-conditional ceasefire framing places Ukraine in adversarial declaration position; no coordinated cessation achieved.
positiveSignals: Ukraine declined to adopt Russia's framing while offering its own framework — proactive humanitarian-window posture; civil-society and Ombudsperson monitoring sustained.
sector_context: Ukraine peace-window emergency re-queue triggered. May 9-10 (post-Victory Day) verifies whether either declared ceasefire produced field-level cessation. If both hold, this constitutes a de facto short-term cessation that warrants positive AWR/BND signal in next cycle. If either is violated, that strengthens the bad-faith-ceasefire-pattern documentation.
open_actions_resolved: Competing-ceasefire structure documented as signal, not resolution
Ukraine's own framework declaration captured under AWR/INT notation
open_actions_carried: May 9-10 emergency re-queue: verify whether either ceasefire held in practice
Document any documented violations during May 5-9 window
Cross-reference Russia entity for counter-party scoring
